question.text: From FY 2024-25, which tax regime is the default for individual taxpayers?

answer.text: Old tax regime with all deductions available
The taxpayer must mandatorily choose a regime every year; there is no default
New tax regime is the default; the taxpayer must actively opt out to choose the old regime
Old regime for salaried individuals and new regime for business income earners

explanation.text: **The Logic:** From FY 2024-25 (AY 2025-26), the new tax regime under Section 115BAC is the default regime for all individual taxpayers and HUFs. If a taxpayer wishes to continue with the old regime, they must specifically opt out by filing Form 10-IEA before the due date of filing the return. Failure to opt out means the new regime applies automatically. **The Trap:** Option (a) was true prior to FY 2024-25 when the old regime was default. Option (b) is wrong — there is indeed a default now. Option (d) incorrectly segments the default by income type — the default applies uniformly to all individuals. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and X-B both test awareness of default regime changes for advising clients on tax-saving product selection. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Many clients are unaware that the new regime applies by default — advisers must proactively verify and guide clients on regime selection before the return filing deadline.
